0_Overview: What is System Design: Designing Scalable, Reliable, Maintainable Systems
Why System Design Matters: Real-world Architecture Decisions
System Design Interview Framework: Requirements → Estimation → Design → Deep Dive
Functional vs Non-functional Requirements
Back-of-the-envelope Estimation: QPS, Storage, Bandwidth
Scalability: Handling 10x, 100x, 1000x Growth
Reliability: Systems That Work Correctly Even When Things Fail
Availability: Uptime SLAs — 99.9% (8.7h/yr), 99.99% (52min/yr), 99.999% (5min/yr)
Maintainability: Operability, Simplicity, Evolvability
Trade-offs: Consistency vs Availability, Latency vs Throughput, Cost vs Performance
Key Components: Load Balancers, Caches, Databases, Message Queues, CDNs
Stateless vs Stateful Services
Synchronous vs Asynchronous Communication
Push vs Pull Architecture
Monolith vs Microservices: When to Split
Data Partitioning Strategies: Horizontal, Vertical, Functional
Replication: Why and How
Caching at Every Layer: Client, CDN, API, Database
Rate Limiting and Throttling
Famous System Design Resources: Designing Data-Intensive Applications (DDIA)
1_Fundamentals: What is System Design and Why it Matters
CAP Theorem: Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance
2_Networking_Basics: CDN (Content Delivery Network): How it Works
WebSockets vs Long Polling vs SSE
3_Load_Balancing_and_Proxies: Load Balancing Algorithms: Round Robin, Least Connections, IP Hash, Weighted
Nginx as Load Balancer and Reverse Proxy
AWS Elastic Load Balancer (ALB, NLB, CLB)
4_Caching: Cache Eviction Policies: LRU, LFU, FIFO, TTL
Distributed Caching with Redis and Memcached
Cache Stampede and Thundering Herd Problem
5_Databases_at_Scale: SQL vs NoSQL: When to Use Which
6_Message_Queues_and_Streaming: Apache Kafka: Topics, Partitions, Consumers, Producers
Kafka: Consumer Groups and Offset Management
7_Microservices: Inter-service Communication: Sync (REST, gRPC) vs Async (Events)
8_Storage_Systems: Block Storage vs Object Storage vs File Storage
AWS S3: Buckets, Objects, Presigned URLs
9_Design_Patterns: Rate Limiting: Token Bucket, Leaky Bucket, Fixed Window, Sliding Window
10_Classic_System_Design_Problems: Design a Video Streaming Service (like YouTube/Netflix)
Design a Distributed File Storage (like Google Drive)
Design a Metrics and Monitoring System
11_Cloud_and_DevOps: Cloud Computing Models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
AWS Core Services: EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, CloudFront
Serverless Architecture: AWS Lambda, Vercel Functions
CI/CD Pipelines: GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI
Centralized Logging: ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana)

0_Overview: What is Computer Science: The Study of Computation, Algorithms, and Data
Core CS Pillars: Algorithms, Data Structures, Operating Systems, Networks, Compilers, Databases
Why CS Fundamentals Matter for Software Engineers
Abstraction Layers: Hardware → OS → Runtime → Application
Von Neumann Architecture: CPU, Memory, I/O
Binary, Hexadecimal, and Number Systems
Boolean Logic and Logic Gates
Data Representation: Integers, Floats (IEEE 754), Characters (ASCII, Unicode)
Memory Hierarchy: Registers → L1/L2/L3 Cache → RAM → Disk
CPU Execution Cycle: Fetch, Decode, Execute
Concurrency vs Parallelism
Synchronization Primitives: Mutex, Semaphore, Monitor
Networking Fundamentals: OSI Model, TCP/IP, HTTP
Compiler vs Interpreter vs JIT
Formal Languages: Regular, Context-Free, Turing-complete
Computability: Halting Problem, P vs NP
Complexity Classes: P, NP, NP-Complete, NP-Hard
Information Theory: Entropy, Compression, Encoding
Cryptography Basics: Symmetric, Asymmetric, Hash Functions
CS Degree Core Courses: Algorithms, OS, Networks, Databases, Compilers, Theory of Computation
1_Operating_Systems: CPU Scheduling Algorithms: FCFS, SJF, Round Robin, Priority
Virtual Memory and Page Replacement Algorithms
Inter-Process Communication (IPC): Pipes, Sockets, Shared Memory
2_Computer_Networks: IP Addressing: IPv4, IPv6, Subnetting, CIDR
TCP: Three-way Handshake, Flow Control, Congestion Control
3_Compiler_Design: Phases of a Compiler: Lexical, Syntax, Semantic Analysis
